title: Gentle Simmered Ethiopian Lentil Stew
description: A fresh and tender Ethiopian dish featuring garlic powder and nuka-pickle, prepared in a hot pot vessel, suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and drain well.
Heat the hot pot vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened.
Add the garlic powder, nuka-pickle, berbere spice, and salt, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lentils, vegetable broth, and tomato paste, and stir well.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
